title: invalid version displayed in the pkg installer of v4.6.0-RC1
body:
### Issue Description

I've downloaded https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.6.0-rc1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg

When I run it, it displays 4.5.0
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/436777/961e588c-b945-4576-aa5d-6cb00e6567d7)



### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Download the pkg and execute it

you can also check by unpacking the files

```
pkgutil --expand  ~/Downloads/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg /tmp/pkg
more /tmp/pkg/Distribution                                                                                                      ✔  8s 
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<installer-script minSpecVersion="1">
    <title>Podman 4.5.0</title>
    <background mime-type="image/png" file="banner.png" scaling="proportional"/>
    <welcome file="welcome.html" mime-type="text/html"/>
    <conclusion file="conclusion.html" mime-type="text/html"/>
    <license file="LICENSE.txt"/>
    <options customize="never" hostArchitectures="x86_64,arm64"/>
    <domains enable_localSystem="true"/>
    <choices-outline>
        <line choice="podman"/>
    </choices-outline>
    <choice id="podman" title="podman">
        <pkg-ref id="podman.pkg"/>
    </choice>
    <pkg-ref id="podman.pkg" packageIdentifier="com.redhat.podman" version="4.5.0" installKBytes="396521">#podman.pkg</pkg-ref>
    <pkg-ref id="podman.pkg">
        <bundle-version/>
    </pkg-ref>
</installer-script>
```

### Describe the results you received

4.5

### Describe the results you expected

4.6.0-RC1

### podman info output

```yaml
no
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @benoitf!

@lsm5 can you take a look?
@benoitf I think only @ashley-cui will be able to fix this and she will be back tomorrow. So, if this can't wait until then, would you mind rebuilding the macos installers like you did for v4.5.1
Huh, don't know how that happened. I think maybe I forgot to run `make clean` before building. Anyway, updated and fixed, thanks!
